t_ch: 至于价钱，给朋友的都是友情价，再加折扣券，等于不花钱！
t_en: As for the price... well, we offer friendship discounts. Add that on top of the coupons and you've got yourselves a free deal!

t_ch: 天才的手稿散落在此，看懂手稿的人能成为天才吗？你开始浮想翩翩……
t_en: The genius' manuscripts are scattered here. Can one be a genius too if one can understand their contents? Your mind starts to wander...

t_ch: 天才遗留的手稿散落在桌面上，你试图拿起，但纸页却纹丝不动。上面究竟承载了怎样沉重的知识，你不得而知……
t_en: The genius' manuscripts are scattered over the desk. You try picking them up, but the pages won't budge. What kind of weighty knowledge do the manuscripts carry? You have no means to find out...

t_ch: 桌面上散落着几张手稿残页，你觉得此情此景需要一位会喵喵叫的桌面清扫专家。这突如其来的想法是否又会成为某项实验的灵感来源？你无从得知……
t_en: The remaining pages of a manuscript are scattered all over the desk. In your opinion, the situation calls for a meowing desk cleaning expert. Will that sudden thought become your inspiration for research? You have no means to find out...

t_ch: 空间站的终端网络修好后，越来越多的科员选择远程办公，在某些终端前大排长龙的场面业已消失。
t_en: Since the terminal network of the space station was fixed, more and more researchers have chosen to work from home. The long queues in front of some terminals can no longer be seen.

t_ch: 屏幕分辨率极高，每条数据标注都清晰可见，你开始怀念低分辨率的时代。
t_en: With the extremely high resolution of the screen, every data label is clear. You start missing the era of low resolution.

t_ch: 在仔细阅读了「请提交数据周报」的弹窗提醒后，你试图找熄屏按钮，但不幸的是，你没能找到它。
t_en: After carefully reading the pop-up notification that reminds you to submit the weekly data report, you fumble for the button to turn off the screen but fail to locate it.

t_ch: 静止的画面似乎是空间站接驳车的行程图，它们还能等来下一位客人吗？你很怀疑。
t_en: The still image on the screen appears to be the route map for the space station shuttle buses. Will there still be passengers for them to pick up? You doubt it.

t_ch: 你向下望去，一股强烈的危机感沿着脊椎攀爬至脑后，你默默握紧双拳。
t_en: As you look down, a strong sense of crisis creeps up your spine to the back of your head. You clench your fists in silence.

t_ch: 你扫视着下方，这里是整个禁闭舱段最骇人的区域，但你并不害怕，不会有什么存在比你本人更令人畏惧了。
t_en: You give the area below a scan. It is the most terrifying place in the entire seclusion zone, but you are not scared. Nothing can be more fearsome than yourself.

t_ch: 有传闻称下方区域隐藏着不可告人的秘密，有神秘怪物在此出没。你越发觉得这座空间站并没有外人想象中的那么「正常」。
t_en: Rumor has it that there are untold secrets hidden in the area below and mysterious monsters have been sighted. The more you think about it, the more you realize that this space station is not as "normal" as outsiders think.

t_ch: 冷冻休眠舱，在用不上的时候，它们便沦为维系空间站形象工程的道具。
t_en: The cryosleep pods, when not in use, are tools to maintain the image of the space station.

t_ch: 休眠舱里空空荡荡，你猜测在此休眠的科员或许已经得到了妥善安置。
t_en: Seeing the empty cryosleep pod, you suppose the researcher once inside has been properly accommodated.

t_ch: 很遗憾，童话故事在科学面前并不奏效，你并没有在冷冻舱里找到故事里的「睡美人」。
t_en: Unfortunately, fairy tales aren't a thing in the world of science, and you find no legendary Sleeping Beauty in the cryosleep pod.

t_ch: 密封的圆柱体实验舱，你只在影视作品里见过。
t_en: The sealed cylindrical experimental pod is something you have only seen in movies.

t_ch: 空间站用于进行各种生物实验的密封舱，曾被用来隔离危险物质，你很好奇它究竟装过什么东西。
t_en: The sealed pod is used to conduct various biological research at the space station and was once used to isolate dangerous substances. You wonder what kind of stuff it has contained.

t_ch: 你将脸贴在冰冷的表面向内窥探，却只能看见灯光下倒映出你的脸。你与空空如也的实验舱面面相觑。
t_en: You press your cheeks against its icy surface and peek inside, only to see your own reflection under the lights. You and the empty experimental pod gaze at each other.

t_ch: 绿叶蔬菜在某些星球是遭人嫌弃的土腥食材，在这里倒成了一种美味。
t_en: Leafy greens, which are considered repulsive and backwards foods on some planets, are a delicacy here.

t_ch: 「别摘！」金属外框上贴着界种科留下的便条。
t_en: "Don't take this off!" A note by the Department of Ecology is attached to the metal frame.

t_ch: 「你已进入监控范围，眼看手勿动。」这警告是针对你的吗？从培养田里肆意生长的植物来看，标语牌在这儿停留了很长一段时间，显然是你想多了。
t_en: "This area is under surveillance. Look with your eyes, not your hands." Is this warning meant for you? Judging from the plants growing wildly in the cultivated field, the sign has been here for quite some time. Surely you are overthinking.

t_ch: 界种科为培育出的植物提供了低温与干燥的密闭器具，以确保植物的长时间保存与活性。
t_en: The Department of Ecology keeps the grown plants in low-temperature, dry, and airtight containers to ensure long-term preservation and vitality.

t_ch: 培养舱中的植物形态让你感到似曾相识，它与星际和平公司某款食品的广告图片完全吻合，界种科捞外快的能力令你咋舌。
t_en: The plants in the incubator look familiar, as they closely resemble an advertising image of a food product from the IPC. You are amazed by the Department of Ecology's ability to rake in extra income.

t_ch: 在科员们撤离之后，植物培养舱依然在正常运转。
t_en: The plant incubator has been operating normally since the evacuation of the researchers.

t_ch: 培养罐中孕育着大株绿植，你很好奇它是否需要吸收星光。
t_en: Large green plants are grown in the incubation jar. You wonder whether they need to absorb starlight.

t_ch: 培养罐中的绿植只是露出新芽，但体积已经十分惊人，你很怀疑它们究竟能长到多大。
t_en: Although the plants in the incubation jar are only germinating, they are already of a staggering size. You wonder how tall they can grow into.

t_ch: 古怪的植物似乎无法吸收星光，你将脸贴近培育罐仔细观察，竟隐隐听到说话声，你猜测这可能就是空间站内某些爱说话的植物的雏形。
t_en: It seems that the odd plants are unable to absorb starlight. You lean in closer to the incubation jar for a closer look. To your surprise, you hear the sound of talking. You wonder if they might be the root of some chatty plants found within the space station.

t_ch: 很高兴认识你！
t_en: Glad to meet you!

t_ch: 你也很可爱哇！
t_en: You're very cute too!

t_ch: 我才不要跟你好……
t_en: I don't wanna be friends with you...

t_ch: 呼噜…呼噜呼噜……
t_en: *snore*

t_ch: 你往那边挪点，我没地方睡了。
t_en: Move over. I have nowhere to sleep.

t_ch: 有点冷，我可以趴在你身上睡吗？
t_en: It's quite cold. Can I sleep on top of your body?

t_ch: 好舒服，要是有被子就好啦。
t_en: This is comfy. I wish I had a blanket.

t_ch: *吧唧吧唧…吧唧…*
t_en: *Smacking lips*...

t_ch: 吃得好饱，呼呼！
t_en: All full. Whew!

t_ch: 真受不了吃饭吧唧嘴的混蛋。
t_en: I can't stand those who eat with their mouths open.

t_ch: 嗝——感觉有点不消化，是吃太多了吗？
t_en: *Burp* My tummy's not feeling too good. Did I eat too much?

t_ch: 当前区域空间利用率未达到<unbreak>100%</unbreak>，请考虑提升空间使用率。
t_en: Space utilization rate for this domain is not yet <unbreak>100%</unbreak>. Please consider increasing space utilization rate.

t_ch: 不要的东西可以塞给我哦！
t_en: You can leave anything you don't want with me!

t_ch: 我什么都吃！
t_en: I'll eat anything!

t_ch: 这些东西好难消化——嗝——
t_en: Those are tough to digest *burp*...

t_ch: 要是有相机就好啦！
t_en: Wish I had a camera!

t_ch: 来拍大合照吧！
t_en: C'mon, let's snap a group picture!

t_ch: 这里好像没看到资料室。
t_en: I can't seem to find any archives here.

t_ch: 排队站好，来报个数。
t_en: Stand in line at attention and number off.

t_ch: 你是几号机？我才是一号机！
t_en: Which number are you? I'm number one!

t_ch: 好奇怪，为什么大家都说我们身上有妈妈的味道……
t_en: That's weird. Why does everyone say that we smell like Mama's Kitchen...

t_ch: 有什么好吃的糕点吗？
t_en: Any yummy cakes?

t_ch: 三缺一速来！
t_en: We're one player short. Hurry over!

t_ch: 你手气也太好了吧？我不信，一会儿换个位置再来。
t_en: That's a hot hand you got tonight! I can't believe it. Will you switch places with me?

t_ch: 「杠上开花」算什么，我刚刚还自摸清一色了呢。
t_en: "Dead Wall Draw"? That's nothing. I just drew a Pure Suit.

t_ch: （沉默不语）
t_en: (Silence)

t_ch: 我们有三名…伤口倒是有六个……
t_en: There's three of us... But six wounds...

t_ch: 真巧，你也叫克拉拉吗？
t_en: What a coincidence. Is your name also Clara?

t_ch: 你们有见到一个高高的机器人吗？
t_en: Have you guys seen a tall robot around here?

t_ch: 咱们叠起来，一个顶三个…哎唷你打到我尾巴了。
t_en: Let's stack them up, four a stack... Ack, you hit my tail.

t_ch: 我感觉我们可以去表演杂耍，以我们的天赋——绝对有搞头！
t_en: I feel like we could put on our own variety show. With our talents, we'll definitely make a good show!

t_ch: 你可以再表演一下那个吗……
t_en: Can you do an encore of that one...

t_ch: 你怎么也在？
t_en: What are you still doing here?

t_ch: 我是来见{F#她}{M#他}的。
t_en: I'm here to see {F#her}{M#him}.

t_ch: 嘘，现在该听我说啦。
t_en: Hush. It's my turn to speak.

t_ch: 你们该不会都有什么隐藏的天赋吧？
t_en: Don't tell me you all have some kind of hidden talent?

t_ch: 接下来要去哪里冒险呢？
t_en: Where to for the next adventure?

t_ch: 或许我们可以推进一个项目，模拟出一个微缩宇宙……
t_en: Maybe we can advance the project and simulate our own nano-universe...

t_ch: 这个概念很不错，可惜以你和我的体量，恐怕不能做到尽善尽美……
t_en: It's a brilliant concept. What a shame that with our size we won't be able to get things perfect...

t_ch: 好啦，别吵啦，都给人家看无语了。
t_en: Fine, quit making such a ruckus. People are going to stare.

t_ch: 你的花花好好看，在哪里搞到的？
t_en: Your flowers are gorgeous. Where'd you get them?

t_ch: 我听说那里有一个房间，里面种了很多花花！
t_en: I heard there's a room there with lots and lots of flowers planted inside!

t_ch: 你这伤口不要紧吧？外面是不是很危险？
t_en: Is your wound okay? Is outside really dangerous?

t_ch: 嘘，吾在炼心，保持安静。
t_en: Shh, I'm training my mind. Keep quiet.

t_ch: 你也是被选中的孩子吗？
t_en: Are you a chosen child, too?

t_ch: 听说垃圾桶里藏着身怀绝技的{F#老奶奶}{M#老爷爷}，不知道{F#她}{M#他}能不能收我为徒……
t_en: I heard about this mystical old master {F#granny}{M#grandpa} inside the trashcan. I wonder if {F#she}{M#he} would ever take me on as a disciple...

t_ch: 呀——我们的颜色都是一样的！
t_en: Ah, our colors match!

t_ch: 当初阮•梅女士创造我们时，用的一定是同一袋面粉。
t_en: Miss Ruan Mei must've used the same bag of flour when she was creating us.

t_ch: 我头顶的颜色是天生的，你这个…是在哪里染的吗？
t_en: The color on top of my head is natural. Did you get yours dyed somewhere?

t_ch: 我身上也有这种「胎记」，我们会不会是失散多年的兄妹？
t_en: I've got that kind of "birthmark" on my body, too. Could we really be long-lost siblings?

t_ch: 没想到还能遇到和我脾气一模一样的家伙……
t_en: Never thought I'd ever meet someone with exactly the same temper as me...

t_ch: …我是在照镜子吗？你走两步我看看。
t_en: ...Am I looking in the mirror right now? Step back a bit and give me a proper look.

t_ch: 你努力踮起脚尖，试图看懂屏幕上的数据与理论。但你目光所及之处，只有空白的墙壁。
t_en: You stand on tiptoe, trying to read the data and theories on the screen. Yet, within your vision exist only blank walls.

t_ch: 你很懊恼，这些高深的理论离常人如此遥远。墙壁空空如也，就像你放空时的大脑，你的思绪飘远……
t_en: You are left frustrated by those profound theories which seem unreachable by average people. The walls are blank, just like your mind when you are spacing out. Your thoughts are drifting away...

t_ch: 你忽然醒悟，为什么要徒劳地追逐理论的彼岸，你该让宇宙的法则为你臣服。
t_en: Suddenly, realization dawns on you. Why make futile efforts to prove theories when you should in fact make the laws of the universe yield to you?

t_ch: 你本以为凭你的身高，想看清屏幕上的所有数据与理论都不在话下。但等你站定，你惊觉自己的渺小。
t_en: You thought you could easily read all the data and theories on the screen with your height. However, when you get to your feet, you find to your utter consternation how small you are.

t_ch: 你很疑惑，空间站为何要将屏幕挂在这么高的地方，你的思维开始逸散……
t_en: You are puzzled as to why the screen is hung so high up in the space station. At the same time, your thoughts begin to wander...

t_ch: 你从漫无边际的想象中猛地醒过来，你所窥见的，仅仅是整座空间站理论成果的冰山一角…想到这里，你对空间站肃然起敬。
t_en: You snap out of your boundless imagination and realize what you have peeked is merely the tip of the iceberg of the entire space station's theoretical results... The realization leaves you in awe of the space station.

t_ch: 「独家发售，稳定更新！」广告机无声地展示着黑塔手稿即将发售的消息。
t_en: "Exclusive release with regular updates!" Information about the upcoming sale of Herta's manuscripts is silently displayed on the advertising bot.

t_ch: 空间站终端的网络连接已经恢复，但广告机音响的修复工作还未提上日程。
t_en: The network connection of the space station terminal has been fixed, but the repair of the advertising bot's audio system is nowhere on the agenda.

t_ch: 你开始想念黑塔的声音，你有些好奇，她真的能「稳定更新」吗？
t_en: You are starting to miss Herta's voice, and you wonder whether she can really manage "regular updates" for her manuscript.

t_ch: 「新品上市，罐装一切！」广告机无声地展示着罐头广告，它的音响似乎还未修复。
t_en: "New product in the market! Everything can be canned!" An advertisement for a canned product is silently displayed on the advertising bot. Its audio system has yet to be fixed.

t_ch: 你认真研读了广告词上的「罐装一切」字眼，你怀疑这和奇物「分装快乐」有些关联，毕竟它们都是「罐头」。
t_en: You scrutinize the "Everything can be canned!" part of the advertisement and suspect it to have some association with the Joy Filtration Set Curio, since they are both about cans.

t_ch: 「定制化的绿色生活。」广告机无声地展示着绿色食品广告，它的音响似乎还未修复。
t_en: "Customize your eco-friendly lifestyle!" An advertisement for an environmental-friendly food product is silently displayed on the advertising bot. Its audio system has yet to be fixed.

t_ch: 你认出了广告画面上的界种科标识，你有些好奇——不知回到主控舱段后，能不能问界种科的科员要几盒来尝尝……
t_en: Noticing the Department of Ecology logo on the advertisement, you wonder whether you can get some samples of the product from their researchers when you return to the master control zone...

t_ch: 在科员们撤走以后，全局监控系统仍在运行。
t_en: The overall station surveillance system has been operating normally since the evacuation of the researchers.

t_ch: 屏幕最下方一行小字吸引了你：「系统正等待唤醒，请及时更新虹膜信息。」
t_en: A line at the bottom of the screen catches your attention: "System is waiting to be re-activated. Please update the iris information promptly."

t_ch: 什么人会把「生命培育箱」放在这堆破烂上？
t_en: What kind of person would leave a Life Incubator on a pile of junk like this?

t_ch: ——原来是你，你想起和洞悉者在此发生的一段小插曲，心神不自主地放松了下来。
t_en: So, it was you after all. You recall an episode featuring the Insighter here and your mind automatically relaxes.

t_ch: 你看向「王座」的目光也不自觉柔和了起来，这是你和洞悉者之间的小秘密，你将这段故事埋在心里某处柔软的角落。
t_en: Your gaze at the throne subconsciously softens. This is a little secret between you and the Insighter, and you bury it away somewhere in a warm, fuzzy corner of your heart.

t_ch: 嗯？有什么值得一瞧的东西吗？
t_en: Hm? Are you trying to have a staring contest or something?

t_ch: 听说你找我有事…
t_en: I heard you have something to tell me...

t_ch: 噢，那个啊，你不说我都忘了，实在是件小事……
t_en: Ah, I almost forgot. Well, it's nothing important after all...

t_ch: 别废话，快说。
t_en: Get to the point!

t_ch: 给我有话直说！
t_en: Stop beating around the bush!

t_ch: 我说我说！别生气嘛，不要这么冲动…是这样，我发现自己从来没得到过某样东西，我很想得到它，希望你能帮助我。
t_en: Alright, alright! Calm down, there's no need to get antsy... Here's the thing — I realized there's something that I've never had but always truly wanted. I hope you can help me.

t_ch: 这样东西就是「朋友」。对，我华莱士•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀这一生中，从来没拥有过真心的朋友。
t_en: And that something is... a friend. That's right, I, Wallace Cambridge Limestein, have never had a true friend in my life.

t_ch: 不论花多少钱都行，我想得到朋友这样东西。
t_en: I would pay anything to acquire this thing they call a "friend."

t_ch: 「朋友」不是东西…
t_en: A "friend" isn't a thing...

t_ch: 你说的也有道理，是东西就能买到。
t_en: You're right about that. Things can be bought.

t_ch: 钱买不到真心的「朋友」
t_en: Money can't buy true friendship.

t_ch: 那可以多加点钱，把真心一起买了。
t_en: So you pay extra for the "true" part?

t_ch: 如果你能帮我交到朋友，我一定给你丰厚的酬劳。朋友越多越好。
t_en: If you can help me acquire a friend, I'll reward you handsomely. The more friends the better.

t_ch: 不过别太声张，我华劳斯怎么也是个有头有面的人物…不要闹得满城皆知！
t_en: But keep it discreet — I'm a well-known figure out there... I wouldn't want the whole city in an uproar!

t_ch: 嗯？有什么值得一瞧的东西吗？
t_en: Hm? Are you trying to have a staring contest or something?

t_ch: 我是你的朋友啊！
t_en: I am your friend!

t_ch: 你就是我一直梦寐以求的「朋友」？
t_en: You're the friend I've been craving all this time?

t_ch: 怪了…什么情绪也没有，我还以为自己会很满足，很高兴……
t_en: How strange... I feel nothing. I thought I'd be so satisfied and contented...

t_ch: 开玩笑的，我们还不是朋友。
t_en: Just kidding. We're not friends yet.

t_ch: 原来如此，看来还是得从熟人中寻找朋友……
t_en: I see. I guess I'll just have to seek friends among my acquaintances.

t_ch: 所谓君子之交淡如水…
t_en: As the saying goes: A hedge between keeps friendship green...

t_ch: 我们刚刚认识，完全没有来往嘛。看来还是得从熟人中寻找朋友……
t_en: Well, we've had no prior dealings — we only just got to know each other. I guess I'll just have to seek friends among acquaintances.

t_ch: 嗯？有什么值得一瞧的东西吗？
t_en: Hm? Are you trying to have a staring contest or something?

t_ch: 帮你交朋友…
t_en: About helping you make friends...

t_ch: 啊，说起来，我有个好人选…
t_en: Aha! On that note, I do have a good candidate in mind...

t_ch: 有个叫莉拉的动物学家，学识渊博，了解很多新奇的东西，和我应该很谈得来。请你帮我问问，她愿不愿意和我做朋友？
t_en: There's an erudite zoologist named Lila who understands all manner of novel things. I think we would get along splendidly. Would you mind asking on my behalf whether she's willing to be my friend?

t_ch: 有个叫曼娅的公务员，出身很不错，和我家也有点渊源。请你帮我问问，她愿不愿意和我做朋友？
t_en: There's a civil servant named Manya, who is of good lineage — in fact, our families have a bit of history. Would you mind asking on my behalf whether she's willing to be my friend?

t_ch: 有个叫艾格尼斯的记者，成天在行政区晃悠，感觉挺有意思。请你帮我问问，她愿不愿意和我做朋友？
t_en: There's a journalist named Agnes who wanders around the Administrative District every day. She seems to be an interesting person. Would you mind asking on my behalf whether she's willing to be my friend?

t_ch: 有个叫拉雯妮的房地产中介，跟我做过几笔买卖，地位和我很相配。请你帮我问问，她愿不愿意和我做朋友？
t_en: There's a real estate agent named Lavonne with whom I've had a few dealings — in terms of social status we're an excellent match. Would you mind asking on my behalf whether she's willing to be my friend?

t_ch: 有个叫尼古莱的姑娘，那天搭讪我，问我愿不愿意帮她写信，是不是对我感兴趣？请你帮我问问，她愿不愿意和我做朋友？
t_en: There's a girl named Nika who accosted me the other day and asked me to write a letter for her. Perhaps she's interested in me? Would you mind asking on my behalf whether she's willing to be my friend?

t_ch: 要是找到了什么珍惜的动物物种，记得告诉我喔。
t_en: Remember to tell me if you find any rare animal species.

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 华劳斯？华劳斯是谁？
t_en: Wallace? Who's Wallace?

t_ch: 据说是行政区最有钱的男人
t_en: They say he's the richest man in the Administrative District.

t_ch: 喜欢假装自己很穷的那个
t_en: That guy that likes pretending he's poor?

t_ch: 你说的该不会是华莱士吧？好巧啊，他就是我的赞助商…等等，他要和我做朋友？我还以为他也对物种学感兴趣呢！
t_en: You can't be talking about Wallace? What a coincidence! He's my sponsor... Wait, he wants to be friends with me? I thought he was interested in speciology!

t_ch: 呃，说真的，我们最怕的就是和金主之间产生奇怪的关系了…你说的这件事我就当没听过，好不好？你就说你没找到我，拜托了！
t_en: *sigh* To be honest, the last thing I want is to enter into weird relationships with my backer... I'll pretend I didn't hear this, okay. Please just tell him that you didn't find me!

t_ch: 好吧。
t_en: Alright.

t_ch: 希望你们能成为朋友。
t_en: I hope you two can still be friends one day.

t_ch: 要是找到了什么珍惜的动物物种，记得告诉我喔。
t_en: Remember to tell me if you find any rare animal species.

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 他可是我的赞助商啊…唉，我为什么遇到这样的事情！要是处理不当，出城的计划就泡汤了……
t_en: But he's my sponsor... *sigh* Why do I always get into trouble like this? If I don't get this right, my plan to get out of the city will fall flat...

t_ch: 他为什么会想和我做朋友啊？我们都没说过几句话…我哪里吸引了他的注意，我改还不行吗？
t_en: Why would he want to be friends with me? We haven't talked much at all... What is it about me that attracted his attention? I'll fix it!

t_ch: 他觉得你懂得很多。
t_en: He thinks you're an erudite.

t_ch: 跟他比起来，是很多啦……
t_en: Well yeah, compared to him...

t_ch: 他觉得和你谈得来。
t_en: He thinks he'd get along splendidly with you.

t_ch: 我这边可不这么想呀……
t_en: Well, I highly doubt that...

t_ch: 不行，不行，他太奇怪了！
t_en: No, no. I can't do it. He's so weird!

t_ch: 要是找到了什么珍惜的动物物种，记得告诉我喔。
t_en: Remember to tell me if you find any rare animal species.

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 你又来了啊……
t_en: Here you are again...

t_ch: 是他要我来的。
t_en: He made me.

t_ch: 太不像话了，这个人一点都不在乎别人怎么想吗？我才不会和这种人做朋友！
t_en: This is ridiculous. Doesn't he care how other people might feel? I'll never be friends with people like him!

t_ch: 请你再考虑一下吧！
t_en: Please think about it again!

t_ch: 我拒绝！我莉拉最讨厌那种把私人交往和学术工作混为一谈的人！
t_en: I refuse to! I hate people who confuse academic work with personal relations the most!

t_ch: 我走了，你别有压力…
t_en: I'm leaving. Don't get stressed...

t_ch: 谢谢，我现在确实没有心情思考这些…
t_en: Thanks. I'm just really not in the mood to think about all this...

t_ch: 怎么又来了？都说我很忙，非常忙，城东城西，大事小事，事事都需要我们政务官思考……
t_en: You're here again? I told you I was busy. Like, very busy. No matter where or what the issue is, we civil servants have to be on hand at all times...

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 谢谢，我很感谢这位市民对我的认可，但我还有工作——谁？华劳斯？莱姆士汀家那个？
t_en: Thank you. I appreciate his recognition, but I have work to... Who? Wallace? That Limestein guy?

t_ch: 有意思，没想到我居然有机会认识莱姆士汀家族…
t_en: Interesting. I didn't expect to meet a Limestein...

t_ch: 这么说你同意了？
t_en: I'll take that as a yes?

t_ch: 不对，不能冲动，曼娅啊！你要谨慎！莱姆士汀虽然富有，但本质上只是暴发户，并没有其他贵族的底蕴……
t_en: No, wait! Don't be impulsive, Manya! You have to be careful! The Limesteins are wealthy, but they're also new money. They have no aristocratic roots...

t_ch: 华莱士现年三十二岁又两个月，没有担任任何公职，传闻他整日游手好闲，莱姆士汀家族也不是很喜欢他……
t_en: Wallace is <unbreak>32</unbreak> years and 2 months old. He's never held a public office, and I hear he's a complete idler. The Limestein family doesn't appear to like him much either...

t_ch: 你好了解他啊。
t_en: You know a lot about him.

t_ch: …短期来看，我似乎有机会一跃进入上流社会！但是莱家树敌无数，在政坛缺乏根基。一旦莱家失势，或者华莱士被莱家抛弃……
t_en: ...In the short term, it'd be my expressway to the upper classes! But the Limesteins have so many enemies, and no real foundation in political circles. If they were to lose their influence, or if Wallace were to be disowned by his family...

t_ch: 娘哎，影响仕途啊！
t_en: Gosh, my career would be ruined!

t_ch: 我懂了，再见。
t_en: I get it. Bye.

t_ch: 你俩是挺投缘的。
t_en: I think you two would hit it off.

t_ch: 再见，这位市民！我很感激你带来的口信，只惜我工作在身，没法回应！
t_en: Goodbye, dear citizen! I appreciate your kind message, but I must apologize. I simply have too much work to do to reply!

t_ch: 怎么又来了？都说我很忙，非常忙，城东城西，大事小事，事事都需要我们政务官思考……
t_en: You're here again? I told you I was busy. Like, very busy. No matter where or what the issue is, we civil servants have to be on hand at all times...

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 我的情报果然不假，这一定是个陷阱。莱姆士汀家的公子没理由对我一个小职员这么执着，这背后不知有怎样的政治黑幕…
t_en: As expected, my intel was right all along. This is most certainly a trap. A Limestein has no reason to be obsessed with a simple office worker like me. I wonder what shady political force is driving this...

t_ch: 你想太多了。
t_en: You're thinking too much.

t_ch: 他只是想跟你交朋友。
t_en: He just wants to make friends.

t_ch: 他就是饥不择友而已。
t_en: He's really desperate to make friends.

t_ch: 哈哈，你还是太年轻，思维太浅，格局小了。这世界上没有这么简单的事，一切的背后都有权力和资本的推动……算了算了，我要洁身自好。
t_en: Haha, you're too young and too naive. Nothing is that simple. Everything here is driven by power and capital... Anyway, I choose to preserve my moral integrity.

t_ch: 我好不容易爬到这个位置，我绝不能犯错！
t_en: It took me so long to get where I am now. I can't afford to make a mistake!

t_ch: 怎么又来了？都说我很忙，非常忙，城东城西，大事小事，事事都需要我们政务官思考……
t_en: You're here again? I told you I was busy. Like, very busy. No matter where or what the issue is, we civil servants have to be on hand at all times...

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 我是一名光荣的政务官，我的生活里没有朋友，只有需要服务的市民！再见！
t_en: I am an honorable civil servant. I have no time for friends — only citizens that need to be served! Goodbye now!

t_ch: 随着时间推移，鲑鱼的醇味被稀释，夕红果的肉脯会被入侵，当夕红鱼的食材和调味已经难分彼此，以食材命名的区分方式将失去意义…
t_en: Over time, the rich flavor of Salmon dilutes, as it makes its way into Redsunset's flesh. When you can no longer tell Redsunset Salmon's main ingredient from its seasoning, the dish's name doesn't make much sense...

t_ch: 我在干什么，我不能再这么思考了……
t_en: What am I doing, I really have to stop thinking...

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 华劳斯？他是美食家吗？
t_en: Wallace? Is he a food connoisseur?

t_ch: 不是。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 他是餐厅老板吗？
t_en: Is he a restaurant owner?

t_ch: 不是。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 可能真是…
t_en: Perhaps...

t_ch: 如果他喜爱美食，那我很愿意和他交流！不然的话还是免了，我还要思考下一期专栏怎么编…怎么写呢！
t_en: If he loves gourmet food then I'm willing to get in touch with him! Otherwise, no can do. I still have to work out how I'm going to spin... I mean, write my next column!

t_ch: 随着时间推移，鲑鱼的醇味被稀释，夕红果的肉脯会被入侵，当夕红鱼的食材和调味已经难分彼此，以食材命名的区分方式将失去意义…
t_en: Over time, the rich flavor of Salmon dilutes, as it makes its way into Redsunset's flesh. When you can no longer tell Redsunset Salmon's main ingredient from its seasoning, the dish's name doesn't make much sense...

t_ch: 我在干什么，我不能再这么思考了……
t_en: What am I doing, I really have to stop thinking...

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 又是那个人？他到底是谁啊？
t_en: Him again? Who is he exactly?

t_ch: 他是莱姆士汀家的公子。
t_en: He's a Limestein.

t_ch: 他是个没朋友的可怜人。
t_en: He's a pitiful guy with no friends.

t_ch: 他除了钱以外一无所有。
t_en: He has nothing apart from money.

t_ch: 听上去和我不在一个世界啊，你还是建议他找别人当朋友吧。
t_en: Sounds like someone from a different world to me. Tell him to find someone else.

t_ch: 你回来啦？宜居城郊板块，新的商机新的风口，要不要？
t_en: You're back? We have all kinds of new suburban commercial prospects. Interested?

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 不行。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 好的，再见。
t_en: Alright, bye.

t_ch: 为什么？
t_en: Why?

t_ch: 因为他是我的客户，跟客户只能谈生意，不能谈友情。
t_en: He's my client. I talk business with clients, not friendship.

t_ch: 你这么讨厌他？
t_en: You hate him that much?

t_ch: 我怎么会讨厌华莱士？我可喜欢他了，问题是——如果你跟一个人是朋友，就只能赚他一次钱；做客户，能赚无数次。
t_en: Why would I hate Wallace? I actually like him. The thing is, you can only make money from a friend once. But with a client, you can make as many times as you want.

t_ch: 你回来啦？宜居城郊板块，新的商机新的风口，要不要？
t_en: You're back? We have all kinds of new suburban commercial prospects. Interested?

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 这家伙还挺固执的哈？我看出来了，哈，他也不傻，想和我套近乎……
t_en: This guy sure is persistent. I see... he's not stupid. He's just trying to curry favor with me...

t_ch: 但是不行，老实说，我很喜欢华莱士，但我可不能为了友情损失一位大客户。
t_en: But still, no can do. To be honest with you, I like Wallace, but I can't lose a big client just for the sake of friendship.

t_ch: 客户就不能是朋友吗？
t_en: Can't clients be friends?

t_ch: 当然可以，有的商人能一边称兄道弟，一边把你卖了数钱。但我不是那种商人，我有自己的原则。
t_en: Of course, some businesses can be on intimate terms with you — all the while selling you down the river. But I'm not that kind of businesswoman. I have my principles.

t_ch: 你回来啦？宜居城郊板块，新的商机新的风口，要不要？
t_en: You're back? We have all kinds of new suburban commercial prospects. Interested?

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 唉，你知不知道华莱士和我是什么关系？上个月他和西蒙勋爵打赌输了，托我处理了两套宅子——这一笔买卖我赚了小半年的收入。
t_en: *sigh* Do you even know the kind of relationship I have with Wallace? Last month he lost a bet with Lord Simon, so he got me to sell two of his mansions. I earned nearly half my annual income just from those deals.

t_ch: 你觉得我要是和这样的金主成了朋友，还能赚他那么多钱吗？那还有人性吗？
t_en: Do you think I could make that kind of money if I became friends with guys like him? And would that even be ethical?

t_ch: 不能公私分明吗？
t_en: Can't you separate life and work?

t_ch: 只要活在这世上，就不可能公私分明的。感情会让人失去理性，大多数人没有能力把两者区分开。
t_en: As long as you live in this world, you'll never achieve that. Emotions make you irrational. Most people can't even tell life from work.

t_ch: 这样赚钱也没好到哪里去啊…
t_en: It's not like there's anywhere else to go with all that money...

t_ch: 但我对得起自己啊，我问心无愧就好，管别人呢？
t_en: At least I can live with myself — my conscience is clear. I don't care what others think.

t_ch: 就这样啦！
t_en: So that's that!

t_ch: 有什么事吗？
t_en: What's up?

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 谁？
t_en: Who?

t_ch: 男的，活的，有钱的那个。
t_en: Male? Still alive? ...Rich?

t_ch: 你的标签几乎覆盖了行政区一半的人，住这儿的非富即贵。
t_en: That's over half the population of the Administrative District. Everyone here is well-to-do.

t_ch: 你搭讪让帮忙写信的那个。
t_en: The one you approached to help with your letter.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我几乎让所有人都帮忙写信来着。
t_en: Hahaha, I ask everyone to help me with that.

t_ch: 我现在还挺忙的，抱歉，如果那位华劳斯有什么事情找我，让他自己来吧。
t_en: I'm pretty busy right now, sorry. If that Wallace has anything else to say, tell him to come find me himself.

t_ch: 有什么事吗？
t_en: What's up?

t_ch: 华劳斯想和你做朋友。
t_en: Wallace wants to be friends with you.

t_ch: 噢，现在我知道是谁了，可我俩没什么来往啊。
t_en: Ohh, now I know who he is. But we've never even spoken to each other.

t_ch: 你请他写过信。
t_en: You asked him to help with a letter.

t_ch: 这…也行吧。但我没那个意思，并没想认识他，抱歉。
t_en: That was... Fine. But that's all it was — I'm not interested in getting to know him. Sorry.

t_ch: 你搭过讪。
t_en: You accosted him.

t_ch: 总之我没那个意思，不是想认识他，抱歉。
t_en: Anyway, that's all it was — I'm not interested in getting to know him. Sorry.

t_ch: 他想的有点多。
t_en: He does overthink things.

t_ch: 看的出来，唉，人和人之间怎样才能正确地传递信息呢？我遇到这种事情越多，越觉得笔杆子乏力。
t_en: I can tell. *sigh* What's the best way to convey information to another person? The more I come across questions like this, the more I realize how tired our writers must be.

t_ch: 还是专注于培养优秀的作者吧。
t_en: We should focus on training good writers instead.

t_ch: 嘘——这里边，每首都有故事。有空的话你也多听听呗？
t_en: Shh... Listen, each song has its own story. Maybe you can listen to some when you have the time?

t_ch: 诶诶诶诶诶，别、别踩！我刚扫的！
t_en: Ehhhh! Don't bring the dirt in here! Pom-Pom just swept!

t_ch: 这又脏了，都是你干的好事！
t_en: It's dirty again all thanks to you!

t_ch: 虽然我没法出去，但就像这样看看外面的星空，好像也挺不错的。
t_en: Pom-Pom can't go outside, yet even from here, the stars are still so pretty to look at.

t_ch: 你说…外面的世界，是什么样的呢？
t_en: So... the outside world. What do you think it's like?

t_ch: 看来暂时是不用再补货了。
t_en: Looks like the inventory won't need be restocked anytime soon.

t_ch: 嗯？来买东西的？记得要先排队哦。
t_en: Hmm? Here to buy something? You'll have to get in line first.

t_ch: 喝完水就要快点长高高帕！
t_en: More water for you to grow taller, taller, taller!

t_ch: 你也需要浇水吗？看你长这么高，不至于吧？
t_en: Do you need to be watered too? It looks like you've grown tall enough already.

t_ch: …ZZZZZ…怎么回事，有什么紧急状况吗？
t_en: ...Zzzzz... What's going on? Did something happen?

t_ch: 列车长也是需要休息的，帕姆可不是机器人。
t_en: Pom-Pom isn't a robot! Train conductor needs breaks too!

t_ch: 旅途…梦想…走到最后的人不过寥寥，更多的人只是在见证诞生与破碎……
t_en: Journeys... dreams... few make it to the end. Most people are just bystanders, witnessing birth and brokenness in the world...

t_ch: 没什么，在列车上待久了，总有一天你会明白的。
t_en: It's nothing. Stay on the train long enough and one day you'll understand.

t_ch: 呣呣呣…扣子怎么会扣不上呢……
t_en: Hmmm... how can a button be so stubborn...

t_ch: 你、你可别误会了，甜甜圈这种东西，帕姆一天只吃三个……
t_en: Do—don't get the wrong idea! Pom-Pom only eats three donuts a day...

t_ch: 每天都得打扫一遍，烦死个人了帕！
t_en: Ugh... Every day Pom-Pom has to clean it over again. What a nuisance!

t_ch: 这位乘客麻烦让一让， 你的列车长还要去干活呢！
t_en: Move along, passenger! Your conductor has to get to work!

t_ch: 好像也没什么特别的事情发生。
t_en: Eh, there doesn't seem to be anything special going on.

t_ch: 不过…车厢里这么安静，反而让人有点不习惯呢？
t_en: Hm... the coach is quiet. Maybe a little too quiet?

t_ch: 唔…站久了果然就会想打瞌睡。
t_en: Ah... Standing for such a long time makes Pom-Pom want to take a nap.

t_ch: 不是我自夸，这里的灯光、温度都是我精心设计过的，会犯困倒也正常。
t_en: Not to brag or anything, but the lighting and temperature here are carefully calibrated by Pom-Pom, so it's normal to get sleepy.

t_ch: 啦啦啦啦～嗯？你回来啦！见到乘客安全归来，列车长自然会感到很欣慰的帕！
t_en: La la la la la~ huh? You're back! It's natural for the conductor to feel relieved when their passengers return safely!

t_ch: 难得的闲暇时光，{NICKNAME}乘客要是玩累了的话可以直接休息哦。
t_en: You finally got some leisure time. Don't forget to grab some sleep when you're done wandering about, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 真好啊，要是我能跟你们一起出去看看的话……
t_en: Sounds nice. If only Pom-Pom could go out on expeditions with you...

t_ch: 你怎么突然回来了，是在外面惹上什么麻烦了吗？
t_en: Back already? Did you get into some trouble?

t_ch: 请不要干扰列车长的日常工作！
t_en: Quit being a nuisance! Pom-Pom is working!

t_ch: 呜哇！帕姆最喜欢{NICKNAME}了！
t_en: Aha! One of Pom-Pom's best buddies!

t_ch: 平安无事又一天～啦啦啦啦～
t_en: Another peaceful day... La la la la la⁓

t_ch: 车厢温度给你调好啦，欢迎回来哦！
t_en: Pom-Pom adjusted the cabin temperature for you~ Welcome back!

t_ch: 帕姆喜欢甜甜圈、星穹列车，还有…列车上的所有乘客！
t_en: Pom-Pom loves donuts, the Astral Express, and all the passengers aboard the train!

t_ch: 倒也没有什么特别有趣的事，不过就是感觉…特别舒服！
t_en: Things have been pretty quiet here recently... but at least it's always cozy!

t_ch: 呜啊…车厢里很舒服对吧，这就是家的感觉！
t_en: The Express is so cozy... Just like home should feel!

t_ch: 年轻人，以前没见过你啊。
t_en: Young {F#lady}{M#man}, I've never seen you before.

t_ch: 我初来乍到。
t_en: It's my first time here.

t_ch: 呵呵，人呐，一辈子总得来趟行政区，要不怎么好自称贝洛伯格人呢？你还是挺幸运的，不像我，打小在这长大，住得都麻木了。
t_en: Heh, everyone needs to visit the Administrative District at least once in their lifetime. How would they be able to call themselves a citizen of Belobog if they don't? You're lucky, I've grown numb to this place from growing up here.

t_ch: 俺是老行政区人了。
t_en: I was also born and raised in the Administrative District.

t_ch: 呵呵，你真幽默。行政区本地每家人我都熟悉，可从没见过你。哎～人人都爱往行政区跑，可我们这些本地人啊，都住腻咯。
t_en: Hehe, you're a funny one. I know every local family in the Administrative District, yet I've never seen you before. *sigh* Everyone wants to come to this district, but for us locals, we've grown tired of living here...

t_ch: 我也没见过你啊。
t_en: I've never seen you before either.

t_ch: 你「没见过」我？没见过我华劳斯？呵呵，那你一定不是本地人。
t_en: You've NEVER seen me before? Never seen Wallace? Hehe, then you must not be a local.

t_ch: 我是华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀，莱姆士汀家族第二十五任家主。幸会！
t_en: I am Wallace Cambridge Limestein. I am the <unbreak>25</unbreak>th head of the Limestein family. Pleasure to make your acquaintance!

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你好，华劳斯•凯特博瑞…
t_en: Hello, Wallace Cambrid...

t_ch: 好！咱们这就算认识了。记住，以后你在行政区需要找什么人办什么事，尽管开口问我就行。
t_en: Good! I'd say we're now fairly acquainted. Remember, if you ever need someone in the Administrative District to get something done, just come to me.

t_ch: 对了，你要是在这附近发现了什么好玩的人或事，别忘了叫上我一起研究讨论。人生嘛，就是要及时行乐！钱不是问题，图的就是一个新鲜。
t_en: By the way, if you find anything or anyone interesting, let me know too. Life is all about having fun! Money is no issue, curiosity fuels me.

t_ch: 呵，真是悠闲的一天…你还有什么想问的吗？
t_en: Ahh, what a fine day it is... Is there anything that you want to know?

t_ch: 想问你几个问题…
t_en: I have a few questions...

t_ch: 说来听听。
t_en: I'm all ears.

t_ch: 你很有钱吗？
t_en: Are you rich?

t_ch: 嗨，算不上有钱，将将够用而已。
t_en: Oh, I wouldn't say I'm rich. I'm... comfortable.

t_ch: 行政区这儿有几套私宅，都得雇人打理，还要交土地税。你说租出去吧？又懒得费这个心思，时间也是钱啊。再说，那些宅子里还放了不少古董……
t_en: I have a couple of properties in the Administrative District that require maintenance. I have to pay taxes for them. But I'm far too lazy to rent them out — I mean, time is money! Besides, they're full of priceless relics...

t_ch: 你很喜欢玩？
t_en: You're into ancient relics?

t_ch: 算不上，就是喜欢折腾而已！
t_en: Not exactly. I just like to own them.

t_ch: 比如前阵子，西蒙勋爵告诉我，他弄到了一块特别稀有的金表，听说是第三任大守护者留下的遗物。
t_en: For example, a while back Lord Simon told me he obtained a particularly rare gold watch, said to be a relic left behind by the third supreme guardian.

t_ch: 这种好东西我怎么能错过！所以我提议，拿三套宅子跟他换那块表……
t_en: How could I miss out on such a rarity! So I offered him three houses for it...

t_ch: 我了解了。
t_en: I think I get the picture.

t_ch: 没什么，再见。
t_en: Nothing. Have a good day.

t_ch: 嗯？有什么值得一瞧的东西吗，还是说有事找我？
t_en: Hmm? Is there anything worth my attention, or something you need to talk to me about?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 嗐，别提了。
t_en: Ack, don't mention it.

t_ch: 我前几天花了点小钱——啊，也就是几个古董花瓶的价格——托人搞到了下层区的机械宠物。可惜吧…组装完之后，也就那么回事。
t_en: A few days ago, I spent a small sum of money — as much as the price of a few antique vases. I requested for someone to fetch a mech-pet from the Underworld. Unfortunately... once it was assembled, it was nothing special.

t_ch: 至于下面来的人呢，张口谈政策，闭口谈钱谈生意。我啊，和他们根本聊不来，没劲透了！
t_en: As for the people who come from below, all they talk about is policies, money, and business. There is absolutely nothing I can talk to them about. How painfully mundane!

t_ch: 我就搞不懂了——哎，你说说，这钱到底有啥用啊？怎么大家成天就想着这点东西…庸俗，实在是庸俗……
t_en: I just can't even fathom the point. Can you try to explain to me what's the use of money? How can people spend all day longing after something like this? It's so unrefined, down right vulgar even...

t_ch: 咦？看你这副模样…有点与众不同啊。
t_en: Hmm? You look... special.

t_ch: 什么意思？
t_en: What's that supposed to mean?

t_ch: {F#是长得太美了的意思吗？}{M#是长得太帅了的意思吗？}
t_en: {F#Do you mean I'm very pretty?}{M#Do you mean I'm very handsome?}

t_ch: 唔…除了毛发灰白以外，看上去倒也还是个正常的人类。
t_en: Well... Besides the hair, you appear to be normal.

t_ch: 但你这身衣服并不是这里常见的款式，上面的纹理装饰也很罕见。而且你裸露在外的手指和脸颊，并没有涂抹矿脂的痕迹……
t_en: But the way you dress isn't the norm around here. Those patterns are rarely seen. And there's no trace of petroleum jelly on those clean fingers and face of yours...

t_ch: 对了，和你聊了这么多，你应该没看过《地底动物百科》吧？
t_en: Oh, yes, I've just been rambling on. You probably haven't read the Underground Encyclopedia of Zoology, right?

t_ch: 啊，抱歉，我并不是有意冒犯你。我叫莉拉，是《远行者》月刊的撰稿人，也是一名动物学家。
t_en: Ah, sorry, I didn't mean to offend you. My name is Lila. I'm a writer for the Voyager, and also a zoologist.

t_ch: 你知道吗？贝洛伯格的人种至少有三个不同的起源。但你似乎并不属于其中任何一种，这也是我当时那么吃惊的原因。
t_en: Did you know that humans in Belobog have at least three different origins? But you don't seem to belong to any one of them. That's why I was so surprised back then.

t_ch: 但你看起来好像…不属于其中任何一种，所以我有点好奇。
t_en: But you... you don't seem to be from any of them, so I'm a little curious.

t_ch: 一定是你的错觉。
t_en: You must be mistaken.

t_ch: 对，我是外星人。
t_en: You're right, I'm an alien.

t_ch: 哈哈，我一定是研究到脑子不正常了…唉，自从得到那本书后，我就经常胡思乱想。
t_en: Haha, I must've been researching too much... Ever since I got my hands on that book, my head's been in the clouds.

t_ch: 什么书？
t_en: What book?

t_ch: 就是这本《地底动物百科》，据说是某个银鬃铁卫从地底带回来的。里面记录了好多奇珍异兽：结晶宝石蜥蜴呀、食髓洞螈呀、葬礼虫呀什么的……
t_en: This one, the Underground Encyclopedia of Zoology. I heard a Silvermane Guard brought it from the underground. It describes many strange creatures: Crystalline lizards, geomarrow olms, funeral worms...

t_ch: 胡思乱想？
t_en: In the clouds?

t_ch: 就是这本《地底动物百科》，据说是某个银鬃铁卫从地底带回来的，里面记录了好多奇珍异兽呢。
t_en: This Underground Encyclopedia of Zoology was apparently brought back from the underground by a Silvermane Guard. It talks about a lot of strange creatures.

t_ch: 自打我看完以后，连吃饭都在想象这些动物的模样。
t_en: Ever since I finished the book, I couldn't stop thinking about how these animals must look like.

t_ch: 真想知道结晶宝石蜥蜴、食髓洞螈、葬礼虫之类的生物，究竟是怎么进食、怎么繁衍的呢……
t_en: I really want to know more about these crystalline lizards, geomarrow olms, funeral worms... I want to know what they eat, how they reproduce... everything.

t_ch: 对了，和你聊了这么多，你应该没看过《地底动物百科》吧？
t_en: Oh, yes, I've just been rambling on. You probably haven't read the Underground Encyclopedia of Zoology, right?

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 贝洛伯格有哪些动物？
t_en: What creatures are there in Belobog?

t_ch: 猫，狗，雪地里的野狼和一些抗寒的鸟类…没别的了。其它动物只能去博物馆里看标本，一睹往日模样。
t_en: Cats and dogs. There are wolves and birds in the wild... That's about it. You'll have to check out the museum's specimens to see what other kinds of animals there were in the past.

t_ch: 听说好多年以前，贝洛伯格还有被驯化的熊，野外有冰原狼。不过现在已经看不到了。
t_en: I heard that hundreds of years ago, Belobog had domesticated bears, and direwolves roamed the wild. They're no longer around though.

t_ch: 不过，我相信还有其它物种设法在寒潮中幸存了下来，只是没法证实而已。现在嘛，也只能研究研究各种各样的人类——
t_en: But I believe there are other animals out there that survived the Eternal Freeze. I just have no way to prove it. Now, I have no choice but to study humans...

t_ch: 你为什么不自己去找？
t_en: Why don't you go look for them?

t_ch: 我也以记者的身份出过几次城，但是报导期根本没时间做生态调查。
t_en: I've been outside the city a few times for journalism, but there wasn't enough spare time to do ecological research then.

t_ch: 不过…我最近遇到一个大金主——只要能满足他的要求，他就愿意资助我探险，而且还帮我搞定了出城的批文。
t_en: But recently, I met a benefactor who is willing to finance my expedition as long as his requirements are met. He's even willing to take care of the expedition permits and formalities as well.

t_ch: 我已经在筹备出城的计划啦。
t_en: I'm already making preparations for the expedition.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 没有，等我见到了再跟你说。
t_en: Not yet. But I'll let you know when I see one.

t_ch: 以后你要是有幸见到了什么珍惜的生物，记得要告诉我哦~
t_en: If you see any rare species in the future, remember to tell me about them!

t_ch: 这套书在市面上可买不到，但是非常有意思的动物学科普读物哦。这两本书就送你啦，有空的时候翻翻看吧。
t_en: These interesting books on zoological science can't be found on the market. So, here, take these two books. Read them when you have the time.

t_ch: 以后你要是有幸见到了什么珍稀的生物，记得要告诉我哦~
t_en: If you see any rare species in the future, remember to tell me about them!

t_ch: 嗯？你来啦，是见到了什么珍稀动物吗？
t_en: Oh, you're back. Seen any rare animal species?

t_ch: 还是你有什么关于动物方面的问题，想来找我聊聊？
t_en: Or do you have any zoological questions you'd like to ask me about?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 我过得挺好的呀，就是最近睡得不怎么好。
t_en: I've been doing alright. I just haven't been sleeping so well.

t_ch: 发生什么事了吗？
t_en: Did something happen?

t_ch: 唔…说出来也不怕你笑话，我现在是市政厅派往贝洛伯格外部区域探索的先遣队队员，负责调查那些未知的动植物。
t_en: Oh... I'm worried you'll laugh if I tell you. I am a member of an exploratory vanguard dispatched by the Town Hall to go outside Belobog. I'm responsible for investigating unknown flora and fauna.

t_ch: 不睡觉学猫头鹰？
t_en: So the reason you aren't sleeping is that you want to imitate owls?

t_ch: 「猫头鹰」？那是什么，从来没听说过呢…壁外有这种动物吗？如果是真的，我一定要去好好调查一番。
t_en: Owls? What's an owl? Never heard of it... Is there a creature like that outside these walls? If there really is such an animal, then I must to go and do some research.

t_ch: 啊…想必你还不知道吧？市政厅最近已经在安排人手探索城外啦。听说上下层区也即将恢复正常，再过不久大家都能随意进出了。
t_en: Ah... You probably don't know, right? The Town Hall recently arranged for some people to explore the area outside the city. Supposedly, the Underworld will be returning to normal soon. Pretty soon, everyone will be able to come and go as they please.

t_ch: 市政厅还给我发了一些户外用具——我准备之后去考察下野生动植物的生存环境，然后把它们记录成册！
t_en: The Town Hall gave me some outdoor gear. Once my preparations are complete, I'll go and investigate the habitat of the wild flora and fauna out there. I'm going to record them to make a manual.

t_ch: 所以啊，一想到我的研究有机会取得重大突破，我就兴奋得整夜都睡不着……
t_en: So, whenever I think about how my research has a chance of achieving a major breakthrough, I get so excited that I can't sleep a wink...

t_ch: 抱歉，我很忙。有事请预约，好吗？如果你预约了——呃，现在是我的休息时间，你可以先去窗口取号排队，预计在一个小时后就会轮到你……
t_en: Sorry, I'm busy. Please make an appointment. If you already have an appointment... well, I'm on break, so you can get a number and wait until you are called. It's expected to be your turn in an hour...

t_ch: 你刚刚还说在忙…
t_en: You just said you were busy...

t_ch: 那当然，我们政务官员永远都在忙，总有那么多事情要思考…市民杂务就像千条线，能穿上线的就只有我们几根针，你说能不忙吗？
t_en: Of course, administrative staff are always busy. There are always so many things to think about... Admin works are like messy yarns, and there are only a few administrators to gather the threads into skeins. How could I not be busy?

t_ch: 哪怕是休息时间，我脑子里也在一刻不停地规划各种工作的细节。所以，请不要打扰我的工作，谢谢。
t_en: I'm going through work details in my brain even during my breaks. So, please don't interrupt my work, thank you.

t_ch: 但大家都在等你…
t_en: But everyone's waiting...

t_ch: 我的工作就是帮市民解决问题。如果保证不了充足的休息，我就没法高效地帮助市民。
t_en: My job is to help citizens solve problems. If I can't get enough rest, how can I help others effectively?

t_ch: 合理分配工作和休息时间，有利于我们提高工作效率。我能理解大家焦虑的心情，但还请你们耐心等待。
t_en: A reasonable time allotment for both work and break helps improve efficiency. I understand your anxiety, but I'll have to ask you to be patient.

t_ch: 听明白了吗，这位市民朋友？
t_en: Do you understand, my friend?

t_ch: 我还有些想问的。
t_en: There's something else I want to ask.

t_ch: 都说了，现在是休息时间…好吧，既然你执意要问，那就问吧。
t_en: I just said I'm on break... Fine, if you insist.

t_ch: 你负责哪一块的公务？
t_en: What kind of work do you do exactly?

t_ch: 问得好！我的工作可能有点琐碎，但却是贝洛伯格正常运转中不可或缺的重要环节！你甚至可以说，我是这座城市的无名英雄。
t_en: Excellent question! My job involves a little bit of everything, but vital to keeping things running smoothly in Belobog! You could say I'm the unsung hero of the city.

t_ch: 我负责行政区的街道和社区事务，倾听来自基层民众的请求，解决日常的民生难题。
t_en: I'm responsible for grassroots affairs in the Administrative District, listening to basic requests from people, and solving day-to-day issues.

t_ch: 那你会倾听我的请求吗？
t_en: In that case, can you hear out my request?

t_ch: 当然没问题！不过，刚刚我说的还不够严谨：每天上午9点到中午<unbreak>12</unbreak>点，下午2点到5点，我在这段时间内负责行政区街道和社区事务。
t_en: Of course! But you will need to reserve a slot. My work hours are from 9:<unbreak>00</unbreak> AM to <unbreak>12</unbreak>:<unbreak>00</unbreak> PM, and 2:<unbreak>00</unbreak> PM to 5:<unbreak>00</unbreak> PM. I handle administrative and civil affairs during these times.

t_ch: 现在是休息时间。作为公职人员，公私分明是最基本的素养。
t_en: I'm currently on break. As a public official, separating professional and private time is of basic importance.

t_ch: 如何预约公务处理？
t_en: How do I make an official appointment?

t_ch: 在克里珀堡二楼<unbreak>236</unbreak>号柜台填写申请表格。然后去克里珀堡三楼<unbreak>35</unbreak>号窗口找工作人员确认盖章。
t_en: Please fill out a form at counter <unbreak>236</unbreak> on the second floor of Qlipoth Fort. Then have a staff member stamp it at window <unbreak>35</unbreak> on the third floor.

t_ch: 盖完章之后，请去克里珀堡一楼的<unbreak>155</unbreak>号窗口排队领预约证明，正式处理通常需要在预约发起一个月以后。
t_en: After stamping, please head to window <unbreak>155</unbreak> on the first floor and wait in line for the proof of appointment. The formal processing usually takes one month after the appointment has been made.

t_ch: 具体的流程请在预约时咨询窗口办公人员。
t_en: To inquire about the specific processes, please consult the window staff when making the appointment.

t_ch: 你其实就是在偷懒而已吧…
t_en: You're just trying to slack off, aren't you?

t_ch: 你怎么会这么想？不过也不怪你，很多人都有和你一样的错误认知，以为我的做的是一份清闲的工作。
t_en: What would make you think that? But I don't blame you. Many have the same misunderstanding, thinking this is just a job where you can kick your feet up.

t_ch: 比如说「上班就是喝茶看报，跟同事聊聊天，然后看着时钟等着下班」。实际上，这都是误传。
t_en: A job where I just "Clock in, drink tea, read the newspaper, chit-chat with colleagues, and stare at the time, waiting to get off." But that's all nonsense.

t_ch: 请抛弃你对基层公职人员的刻板印象，我们的一天可是很忙碌的。
t_en: Please lose these false impressions of grassroots public officials. Our days are busy ones indeed.

t_ch: 没什么事了。
t_en: Nothing else.

t_ch: 没什么了…你继续休息吧。
t_en: Nothing... Sorry to bother you during your break.

t_ch: 不客气，为每位市民服务是我的职责所在。
t_en: It's okay. It is my duty to serve every citizen.

t_ch: 好了，现在请给我一点私人时间……
t_en: Now, if you could just give me some privacy...

t_ch: 怎么又来了？都说了现在是休息时间…哎，这位市民，你到底想问什么事？
t_en: You again? I just said I'm on break... Fine, so what exactly do you want to know?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 过得怎样？如你所见，还是老样子。办着一样的公事，领着一样的工资。
t_en: How have things been? As you can see, it's just the same old, same old. Doing the same work for the same pay.

t_ch: 市政厅没派你去下层区吗？
t_en: Didn't the Town Hall send you to the Underworld?

t_ch: 市政厅已经安排了合适的人员，我们政务官员当然要依照规章制度来行事。
t_en: The Town Hall has already allocated suitable personnel for that. Our administrative officials naturally want to do things by the book.

t_ch: 还有，下层区的民生问题并不在我的职责范围内。你要想咨询下层区的事务，请咨询下层区临时政务官员。
t_en: Also, the livelihoods of people in the Underworld is not within my job responsibilities. If you want to inquire about the Underworld, please submit your inquiries to an Underworld interim administrative official.

t_ch: 噢，我以为你升职了呢。
t_en: Oh, I assumed you had been promoted.

t_ch: …让你失望了，我还是政务部官员，依旧负责处理行政区的民生事务。
t_en: ...Sorry to disappoint you. I am still an Administrative Official responsible for handling matters related to civil affairs in the Administrative District.

t_ch: 大守护者阁下正在优化市政厅的官员结构。但在最终结果公示出来之前，请不要相信任何小道消息，一切以克里珀堡张贴的公告为准。
t_en: Her Excellency, the Supreme Guardian, is optimizing the structure for officials in the Town Hall. But I advise you not to give credence to any gossip before the final results are announced. Everything will be subject to notices posted in Qlipoth Fort.

t_ch: 咳咳，这位市民…没事的话请不要妨碍我工作。为了保证上下层区的正常连通，市政厅上下最近都在加班，我可没时间和你闲聊。
t_en: Ugh. Citizen... If there is no problem, then please refrain from obstructing my work. In order to ensure the regular connection between the Overworld and Underworld, Town Hall officials at every rank are working overtime. I don't have time for idle chatter.

t_ch: 我捡到了几封未妥投的信。
t_en: I found some unsent letters.

t_ch: 投丢的信？这倒是属于政务部的业务范围不假，但是……
t_en: Unsent letters? This falls within the scope of duties of the Administrative Department, but...

t_ch: 唉…行吧。拿给我看看。
t_en: Alas... fine. Let me take a look.

t_ch: 交出未妥投的信封•一
t_en: Hand over the Misdelivered Letter 1

t_ch: 这上面写的什么？「帮我买张邮票贴上，再帮我寄出去」……
t_en: What does it say? "Please buy me a stamp and post this for me..."

t_ch: 那你倒是写上地址呀——这人怎么没头没脑的？
t_en: Why isn't the recipient's address provided? The sender is so careless.

t_ch: …咳，辛苦你了，这封信就交给我处理吧。
t_en: ...Ahem, thanks for doing this. I'll take care of this letter.

t_ch: 你捕捉到曼娅的目光扫了一眼旁边的垃圾桶。
t_en: You notice Manya's fleeting glance at the trashcan next to her.

t_ch: 交出未妥投的信封•二
t_en: Hand over the Misdelivered Letter 2

t_ch: 「行政区，六号街，三十五栋B<unbreak>03</unbreak>室，卡隆•艾尔收。」
t_en: "Carlo Eyre, B<unbreak>03</unbreak>, Building <unbreak>35</unbreak>, 6th Street, Administrative District."

t_ch: 奇怪，地址和收信人写的很清楚，这怎么还会寄丢？邮政局的人也太懈怠了吧……
t_en: How can this letter be undelivered when the recipient's name and address are clearly written on it? The people at the post office must be slacking off...

t_ch: …咳，辛苦你了，这封信就交给我处理吧。
t_en: ...Ahem, thanks for doing this. I'll take care of this letter.

t_ch: 交出未妥投的信封•三
t_en: Hand over the Misdelivered Letter 3

t_ch: 「致我唯一的，珍贵的，芳华永驻的，聪慧伶俐的……」
t_en: "To my one and only beautiful and smart..."

t_ch: …什么乱七八糟的，这人不会觉得自己很浪漫吧？
t_en: ...What kind of nonsense is this? Does this guy think he's very romantic?

t_ch: …咳，辛苦你了，这封信就交给我处理吧。
t_en: ...Ahem, thanks for doing this. I'll take care of this letter.

t_ch: 你捕捉到曼娅的目光扫了一眼旁边的垃圾桶。
t_en: You notice Manya's fleeting glance at the trashcan next to her.

t_ch: 交出未妥投的信封•四
t_en: Hand over the Misdelivered Letter 4

t_ch: 「我写的垃圾的文字就不该见到阳光。也好，就让它们在黑不见底的邮筒里慢慢腐烂吧。」
t_en: "The trash I wrote should never see the light of day. That's fine, it can rot forever in the bottom of the dark mailbox."

t_ch: 我看看…欸…我觉得这人的文笔还不错呀？
t_en: Let me see... Hmm... This person is quite a good writer, huh?

t_ch: …咳，辛苦你了，这封信就交给我处理吧。
t_en: ...Ahem, thanks for doing this. I'll take care of this letter.

t_ch: 你从她的眼神中能看出来，曼娅已经决定把这封信留给自己了。
t_en: You can see in Manya's eyes that she has decided to keep the letter to herself.

t_ch: 「这个甜味，有点像是煦日节之后，冻河初开，从冰块的缝隙中挤出的第一滴水，凛冽之中又裹挟着一丝温暖……」
t_en: "This sweet taste is like the first drop of water from the cracks of the ice when the river unfreezes after the Solwarm Festival, a bit of warmth amidst the bitter cold..."

t_ch: 你觉得这样写怎么样，会让你食欲大振吗？
t_en: What do you think? Doesn't this description give you an appetite?

t_ch: …你在问我？
t_en: ...Are you asking me?

t_ch: 对呀，这儿还有别人吗？
t_en: Yeah, do you see anyone else?

t_ch: …可能会吧？
t_en: ...Maybe?

t_ch: 语气这么勉强？说明你其实就是在说「不」。
t_en: You don't sound so convinced. I understand, you are saying "No."

t_ch: 绝对没戏。
t_en: Not a chance.

t_ch: 唉，也不能怪你，是我的描述还不够吸引人。
t_en: Well, I can't blame you — it's my own problem. My writing is not good enough.

t_ch: 可恶，要是不能用文字构筑味觉、形成通感，那我们美食记者还有什么存在的价值？对了，正巧跟你聊着，我这里还有一段，你帮我听听——
t_en: Darn it. What's the point of being a food journalist if I can't use words to construct a sense of taste and utilize synesthesia? By the way, while I'm talking with you, I have another paragraph here. Let me read it to you.

t_ch: 对了，正巧跟你聊着，我这里还有一段，你帮我听听——
t_en: By the way, I've got another line, listen to this:

t_ch: 「喧宾夺主的浓油赤酱，就像大军入侵一般，将鲑鱼本来的清爽味道完全淹没，那一丝气息微薄的鱼本味，只能高举降旗败下阵来……」
t_en: "The rich sauces are like an invading army, completely overwhelming the crisp and fresh taste of the salmon. The gentle aroma of fish has no choice but to surrender in defeat..."

t_ch: 感觉如何？是不是有点累赘？
t_en: How's that feel? A little too wordy?

t_ch: 你应该去写小说…
t_en: You should write a novel.

t_ch: 的确废话很多。
t_en: Yes, a lot of unnecessary details.

t_ch: 是啊，职业病。我以前是文学记者，每天就是变着法地往句子里塞形容词，要是读者能在三行内弄清楚主谓宾，主编就要扣我奖金。
t_en: I can't help myself, I used to be a literary journalist. Every day I was trying to add more adjectives to the sentence anywhere I could fit them. I wouldn't get my bonus if readers can easily deduce the subject, predicate, and object within three lines.

t_ch: 后来我就转行了，因为来了个新人，比我还会凑字数…哎，你看过《水晶日报》的美食专栏吗？专栏作者艾格尼斯就是我。
t_en: But then a new girl came and was better at filling in words than I was, so I made a career change. Have you read the cuisine section of the Crystal Daily? I am the author of that section.

t_ch: 说实话，我也不想闭着眼睛搞这些虚头巴脑的东西了……
t_en: Honestly, I don't want to include all that garish garbage in my writing anyway...

t_ch: 哎，我打算再研究研究遣词排句的技巧。你还有别的事吗？
t_en: I'm gonna put more work into honing my rhetorical skills. Well, enough about me — how can I help you?

t_ch: 关于写作…
t_en: About writing...

t_ch: 嗯？说来听听。
t_en: Hmm? Please elaborate.

t_ch: 何不试试白描手法？
t_en: Then why don't you try a more straightforward style of writing?

t_ch: 不行啊，报社有要求，主编有规定：首先字数得给够，这玩意有指标的，我们按字数结工资……
t_en: It won't do. The paper has its requirements and the chief editor has his own. Word count needs to hit quotas, that's for sure. We get paid by words after all...

t_ch: 其次，我们主编的硬性标准就是：高端大气！最好让读者一看就觉得自己没作者懂得多，心里油然而生一股子敬佩之情。
t_en: Other than that, the editor has made it clear that our paper is produced with the educated class in mind. Ideally, our average readership should consider exposure to our publication both a privilege and a chance to learn.

t_ch: 那个新人编辑就特别擅长这一手，她有门绝活，就是把一句话翻来覆去地写，洋洋洒洒几百行，本质上就十个字。
t_en: That newbie is a natural for this. She can write a hundred pages for what could have been described using a few words.

t_ch: 你不想这么写吗？
t_en: Don't you admire her talent?

t_ch: 十个字能说清楚的事，谁又想写几千字呢？不过，从专业的角度来看，这也是一门谋生技术，所以把位置让给尼古莱，我也心悦诚服……
t_en: Who wants to write an essay when it could be done with just ten words? But when you write professionally, it becomes a useful skill. Nika was better at this. That's why I had no objections to giving my position to Nika...

t_ch: 不过——我写不来啊！鄙人真的做不到啊！
t_en: Be that as it may, I just can't force myself! I am not made for this!

t_ch: 努力加强下写作技巧吧。
t_en: Write some more and you'll get the hang of it.

t_ch: 一万字的采访稿，被我生生地扩成了四万字，这已经是极限了！
t_en: I've already bloated a ten thousand-word interview script to forty thousand! That's the best I can do.

t_ch: 想想人类迄今为止的写作技巧都被我拿来堆砌字数，实在是悲哀啊。
t_en: To think that the ways of great writers accrued through the ages are used by me to simply bloat word count... How pathetic.

t_ch: 我明白了。
t_en: I've got it.

t_ch: 希望我的连篇累牍也能像今天的解释一样清楚……
t_en: I hope my long articles can be as clear as the explanation today...

t_ch: 关于美食…
t_en: About food...

t_ch: 有什么推荐吗？
t_en: Any recommendations?

t_ch: 没有。不是气话，就是没有——贝洛伯格就这么大，几百年来就这么些食材翻来覆去地做，再怎么排列组合也做不出新菜色了。
t_en: Nope. I'm not mad at you. I'm simply stating facts. Belobog is not a huge place with a huge variety of food. The same ingredients are used for hundreds of years. Quite literally every possible combination has been used at some point.

t_ch: 不过如果你是外地人…那倒是可以试试夕红鱼。
t_en: But if you are not from around here, Redsunset Salmon might be worth trying.

t_ch: 虽然其它区也有这道菜，但没有哪的食材新鲜程度能和行政区相提并论。第一次尝试的味道绝对让你难以忘怀！
t_en: It's not exclusive to this place, I know. But you can only get the freshest salmon from the Administrative District. Once you've tasted it, you'll remember it for the rest of your life.

t_ch: 你尝过多少种菜肴？
t_en: How many dishes have you tried?

t_ch: 往少了说，四千多种吧——但实际上嘛，贝洛伯格人编菜名是一绝：一条鱼身上可以划出几十种部位，每个部位之间排列组合一下，几百道不重样的菜就诞生了。
t_en: Four thousand, if I'm being conservative. But to be honest, hundreds of these dishes are purely new combinations of old recipes. People in Belobog are masters when it comes to naming their food. We have names for dozens of body parts of fish. When you mix and match, you'll come up with new recipes in no time.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 我不打扰你了。
t_en: Thank you for your time.

t_ch: 哎，别客气。有空的话可以看看我的美食专栏，提点意见。
t_en: No worries. If you'd like, do have a look at my column on food. Comments are always welcome.

t_ch: 要是有任何与美食相关的问题，千万不要客气，直接来问我就好。
t_en: If you have any questions related to food, don't hesitate to come to me.

t_ch: 「随着时间推移，鲑鱼的醇味被稀释，夕红果的肉脯无力阻挡被入侵的命运，当夕红鱼的食材和调味已经难分彼此，以食材命名的区分方式将失去意义…」
t_en: "Over time, salmon's rich taste becomes mild and makes its way into the defenseless Redsunset. When the seasoning and ingredient of Redsunset Salmon melt into each other, the distinction drawn between salmon and Redsunset becomes ever more blurred..."

t_ch: 抱歉，我的稿子还没赶完。今天刚写了一段，结果连我自己都看不下去……
t_en: Sorry, still scrambling to finish my article. Did one paragraph today, but I want to bin the whole thing already...

t_ch: 哎，我打算再研究研究遣词排句的技巧。你有事找我吗？
t_en: Need to put more work into honing my rhetorical skills. How may I help?

t_ch: 最近过得怎么样？
t_en: How have things been?

t_ch: 抱歉，我刚刚在脑海里构思稿子的细节，没注意到你。
t_en: Sorry, I was thinking about the details of something I'm writing. I didn't notice you.

t_ch: 你问我最近啊？挺好的，可以说是前所未有的充实。
t_en: You were asking how I've been recently? Pretty well, but I've been more productive than ever.

t_ch: 又在编虚假的美食故事？
t_en: Are you making up food stories again?

t_ch: 没有的事。说起来，多亏了大守护者的决策——虽然我还没资格申请去下层转悠啦——但上下层区的物资已经开始正常运输了。
t_en: I would never make up a story! Anyway, thanks to the Supreme Guardian's decision — even though I still don't qualify to apply to wander around the Underworld — the flow of goods between the Overworld and Underworld has already started to normalize.

t_ch: 那些蘑菇和可食用苔藓，吃起来真不错。我最近品尝了不少新奇的下层区美食，我现在脑子里可是灵感爆炸，得好好捋一捋才行。
t_en: The mushrooms and edible moss aren't too bad. I've recently sampled several new foods from the Underworld, and now my brain is bursting with inspiration. I have to make good use of it.

t_ch: 你不去下层区采采风？
t_en: Will you go to the Underworld to collect materials?

t_ch: 我倒是想呐…但想前往下层区得去市政厅申请许可，像我这种游客性质的出行需求可没那么容易获得批准。
t_en: I certainly would love to... But you need to apply for permission from the Town Hall to go to the Underworld. Travel requests of a tourism nature like mine aren't as likely to receive approval.

t_ch: 不过现在上下层区的物资已经开始正常运输了，下层区的美食从这里也能买得到——而且价格还很便宜。
t_en: At least goods are already starting to be regularly transported between the Overworld and Underworld. I can buy food from the Underworld here, and the prices are cheap.

t_ch: 我这几天都在品尝下层区的新菜，比如蘑菇和可食用苔藓什么的…味道还真不错。等以后彻底放开了，我得好好下去感受下那里的就餐环境。
t_en: I've been trying new dishes from the Underworld in the past few days. I've had mushrooms, edible moss, and more... The flavor has been pretty good. Once travel between the two areas fully opens up, I simply must go down there and experience the dining environment.

t_ch: 宜居城郊板块，水电暖供应充足，精装修拎包入住，稀缺楼盘，机不可失！这么好的机会，我都恨不得给自己来一套！喂，你！你还在等什么？
t_en: Great location in the outskirt of the city, energy and water already sorted out. For this fully furnished property with fabulous interior design, you need to bring nothing but yourself! High demand, limited supply! I'd want to take one for myself! Hey, you there! What are you waiting for?

t_ch: 我在等你说完呢。
t_en: I'm waiting for you to finish.

t_ch: 还有什么可说的？一桩大便宜就在你的眼前！我上面有人，懂吗？我的内部消息向我透露，这批房子的价格在两年内稳涨不跌。
t_en: What else can be said? The price is unbeatable! I have friends in high places. You get my drift? My sources have told me that the price of this property will only go up in the next two years.

t_ch: <unbreak>30%</unbreak>…不，<unbreak>40%</unbreak>！而且实话跟你说吧，这完全是保守估计。
t_en: An increase of thirty percent... no, forty! To be honest with you, I'm being conservative here.

t_ch: 实际涨幅至少是这个数，明白了吧，你不要被吓到啦。我其实是故意往低了说，不能让太多人知道内幕消息，毕竟——
t_en: The actual number is at least this much. Don't look so scared. See what I mean here? I'm trying to keep it on the down low, because...

t_ch: 我不想买房。
t_en: I'm not looking to buy a property.

t_ch: 年轻人不急着买房，我能理解。但是你想过吗，从投资的角度来看，这可不仅是一座房子，它是不动产！
t_en: Sure, sure, young people are not in a hurry. I get it. Let's put it this way: From the perspective of an investor, we are not talking about a house to live in. It's an asset!

t_ch: 我要是你，就趁这个机会买进，等它价格涨上去了，再出手卖掉，大赚一笔！
t_en: If I were you, I wouldn't miss out on this opportunity. Buy it now, wait for the price to go up and sell it. Easy money!

t_ch: 而且，我向你承诺，你只要投资了，不管有没有人接手，你只要打算卖，我就会按涨幅<unbreak>13%</unbreak>的价格给你挂牌——
t_en: Tell you this, if you buy the property and decide to sell it at some point, no matter if you find a potential buyer, I'll list it for you <unbreak>13%</unbreak> higher than the current price. You have my word.

t_ch: 我跟你说，我可是本地最大的中介商！没有我卖不掉的房子。
t_en: Trust me, I am the best local property agent. There hasn't been a house that I couldn't sell!

t_ch: 可城郊全是雪地啊。
t_en: But the outskirts have only snow-covered fields.

t_ch: 我以为你是个目光长远的人呢。难道你只看眼前吗——
t_en: I took you for someone who can think long-term! Don't be shortsighted.

t_ch: 买房就是一项投资，等到楼盘建好了再买，升值空间就小了。很多人买房都喜欢观望，结果呢，观望着房价就飞涨到压根买不起了。
t_en: A property is an investment. If you wait until the house is built before you buy it, that'll eat into your profit margin. I've seen lots of people who like to wait before making a purchase. By the time they made their decisions, the price had already gone out of their league.

t_ch: 而你呢？你是个聪明的投资者，赶在开盘前投资进场，过几年就能高价卖出大赚一笔。
t_en: You, my friend, look like a shrewd investor. Buy it before it's listed and you'll be raking in money in a few years' time.

t_ch: 现在你再看看，那还是一片平平无奇的雪地吗？那分明是一座静待开掘的金山！
t_en: Now when you look at those fields again, do they still look like snow-covered dust bowls? They're unexplored gold mines!

t_ch: 我还是觉得你在骗人…
t_en: I still think you are a scammer...

t_ch: 说话注意点，我是拉雯妮——行政区谁不认识我？只有蠢材才会分不清机遇和骗局，你不傻，对吧？
t_en: Don't be rude! I am Lavonne. If you ask around, there's not a single soul in the Administrative District who doesn't know my name. Only fools mistake opportunities for fraud. I trust you are not one of them?

t_ch: 而且，大守护者的购房新政听说过没有，你去打听打听，绝不会有假的，白纸黑字清清楚楚。
t_en: Even if you don't trust me, surely you have at least heard about the Supreme Guardian's new housing bill? Go ask around and see it for yourself.

t_ch: 连我都已经购置了两套！我还能自己骗自己吗？
t_en: I myself have bought two properties! I can't be scamming myself, can I?

t_ch: 话只能说到这个份上了，你回去再好好想想，别做会让自己后悔的决定！
t_en: I've probably said enough. Anyway, think about my proposal. Don't make a decision that you will someday regret.

t_ch: 我想再深入了解你的房源。
t_en: I'd like to know more about these properties.

t_ch: 再考虑下去，花店的花都要谢了！要是有什么关于买房的问题，我建议你现在就问。
t_en: It'll be too late if you don't make up your mind soon! If you have any questions regarding the property, I suggest that you ask me now.

t_ch: 可我没攒够买房的钱…
t_en: But I don't have enough money...

t_ch: 我知道你在顾虑什么，我就明说吧，看上这个项目的人很多，钱已经投进去不少了。
t_en: I understand your concern. Let me tell you this, lots of people have already invested in it.

t_ch: 现在只需要付一笔定金，我就能在开发商那儿给你先预定一套。怎么样，很方便吧？现在没钱不是问题，未来还长，早投早发财。
t_en: All I need is a deposit, with which I will be able to secure your place for the property. Sounds good, huh? Money is not the problem here. In the long run, the earlier you make the investment, the more you get in return.

t_ch: 房源信息可靠吗？
t_en: Are you certain about the info of the property?

t_ch: 那当然，我手上很多项目都需要和克里珀堡里的大人物打交道，就连现任的大守护者我也见过几面。
t_en: Of course! I'm working with influential figures in Qlipoth Fort on a number of projects. I've even met the current Supreme Guardian a few times.

t_ch: 细节不能透露太多，这事牵扯太大——你是明白人，应该知道我的意思。我只能说懂的都懂。
t_en: I can't go into details, as many are involved if you see what I mean. I trust we understand each other. All I can say is if you are in, you won't regret it.

t_ch: 让我再想想吧。
t_en: I need some time to process all that information.

t_ch: 能问你一些别的事吗？
t_en: Can I ask you something else?

t_ch: 你问吧，客户永远是我的上帝。
t_en: Ask away. It's always a pleasure to help a customer.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格就你一个地产中介？
t_en: You are the only property agent in Belobog?

t_ch: 严格来说，是行政区唯一的房产中介——我在别的区有几个竞争对手，不过嘛，你看地盘就知道谁更厉害，他们可休想染指行政区。
t_en: Technically, I'm the only one in the Administrative District. I do have a few competitors in other districts, but I'm sure you can tell who deals with bigger clients judging by the areas we operate in. They won't be a threat to me in a million years.

t_ch: 我手头有行政区七百年来的全部房产交易纪录，另外，贝洛伯格八大家族的族史我都倒背如流——靠着手头的这些信息，我才能争取到官员的支持和民众的信任。
t_en: I have data on all property sales in the Administrative District for the past seven hundred years, and I know the nitty-gritty of the history of the Eight Belobog Families. My knowledge has won me the trust of both government officials and regular people.

t_ch: 官员和民众都爱来我这打听房源，跟我做生意。住房对每个人来说都是刚需，而我努力工作就是为了满足这些需求。
t_en: People from all walks of life come to me when they need to find properties. Demand for housing has always been high, and my job is to help people find what they need.

t_ch: 那楼盘到底是怎么回事？
t_en: If so, what's the deal with the not-yet-built property we talked about?

t_ch: 嗐，何必问这么细呢？这是种新的商业模式，叫做「预售」。我先收一笔钱，然后给到开发商去做开发，如果开发失败了，我就把钱退还，成功了则皆大欢喜。
t_en: Nah, you don't want me to bother you with trivial details. What we talked about is a new business model called presale. I take the money from the buyer, send it to real estate company for delivery. If for some reason the property can't be delivered, I'll simply refund the buyer. Everyone ended up happy.

t_ch: 可靠渠道告诉我，下一任大守护者布洛妮娅大人是个温和中立派，所以有超过七成的概率会支持开发新城区的战略，而我是行政区唯一的地产中介……
t_en: I've learned from reliable sources that the next Supreme Guardian Lady Bronya is a moderate centrist. There's a seventy percent chance that she will be in favor of developing new districts. Combine that with the fact that I am the only property agent in the Administrative District...

t_ch: 所以我决定做个成熟的商人，转嫁风险、预支利益。这可是个发大财的机会，你千万别错过了！
t_en: Therefore, I decided to be a smart businesswoman who knows how to spread risk and maintain cash flow. This is a great opportunity if you are looking to make a fortune. Don't miss out!

t_ch: 没别的想问的了。
t_en: That's all from me.

t_ch: 这就完了？不打算预购吗？我告诉你，机不可失，失不再来！
t_en: So soon? Not going to make the purchase? It's a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity!

t_ch: 我赶时间，再见。
t_en: I'm in a hurry. See you later.
